name: Hague Convention 2007 on Child Maintenance + Hague Protocol 2007 (CETS-HCCH 2007)
summary: The Hague Convention 2007 on Child Maintenance (formally Hague Convention on the International Recovery of Child Support and Other Forms of Family Maintenance) is a global supranational meta-framework cross-border-child-maintenance binding-treaty instrument adopted by the Hague Conference on Private International Law (HCCH) — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global binding-treaty framework operating specifically on cross-border-child-maintenance framework (Hague Convention 2007 adopted 23 November 2007 in The Hague + effective 1 January 2013 — formal global binding-treaty framework establishing cooperation framework for cross-border-child-maintenance recovery + recognition + enforcement, distinct from Hague Convention 1980 + 1993 + 1996 frameworks which operate on cross-border-child-abduction-resolution + intercountry-adoption + parental-responsibility frameworks respectively, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global binding-treaty framework operating on formal-cross-border-child-maintenance-specific framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal HCCH post-Hague-1996 child-maintenance elaboration framework (Hague Convention 2007 adopted as global comprehensive supplement to Hague Convention 1996 parental-responsibility framework + UN Convention on the Recovery Abroad of Maintenance 1956 framework — applicable to 49 state parties as of 2026, structurally distinctive globally as the most-developed modern global binding-treaty framework on cross-border-child-maintenance framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Hague Protocol 2007 on Maintenance applicable-law framework (Hague Protocol on the Law Applicable to Maintenance Obligations 2007 adopted 23 November 2007 in The Hague + effective 1 August 2013 — formal HCCH Protocol on applicable-law for maintenance obligations framework, distinct from Hague Convention 2007 which operates on cooperation framework, applicable to 33 state parties as of 2026, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global binding-treaty framework operating on formal-applicable-law-for-maintenance-obligations-specific framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal Central Authority cooperation + Form Database + International Iberian Network IberRed framework (Convention Article 4 Central Authority designation framework + Article 5 Central Authority duties framework + Article 9 application to Central Authority framework + HCCH Country Profiles Database + International Iberian Network IberRed framework — formal HCCH-elaborated cross-border-child-maintenance-cooperation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-children's-rights frameworks operating without formal Form Database + International Iberian Network framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global binding-treaty framework operating formal Form Database + International Iberian Network framework). The Hague Convention 2007 is binding upon 49 state parties as of 2026 + the Hague Protocol 2007 is binding upon 33 state parties as of 2026 — substantial state party + non-state-party framework reflecting growing but limited adoption framework. The Convention + Protocol are silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal cross-border-child-maintenance + applicable-law frameworks operate substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal post-divorce + post-separation cross-border-child-maintenance framework. The Permanent Bureau of the Hague Conference on Private International Law is the apex monitoring framework body; national-level Central Authorities + national-level apex courts operate as administrative + judicial bodies. The Hague Convention 2007 + Hague Protocol 2007 are silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: Hague Convention 2007
statutory_framework.relevance: Hague Convention on the International Recovery of Child Support and Other Forms of Family Maintenance adopted 23 November 2007 in The Hague + effective 1 January 2013 — formal global binding-treaty framework establishing cooperation framework for cross-border-child-maintenance recovery + recognition + enforcement.

statutory_framework.title: Hague Protocol 2007
statutory_framework.relevance: Hague Protocol on the Law Applicable to Maintenance Obligations 2007 adopted 23 November 2007 in The Hague + effective 1 August 2013 — formal HCCH Protocol on applicable-law for maintenance obligations framework.

statutory_framework.citation: UN Convention on the Recovery Abroad of Maintenance 1956 (predecessor framework)
statutory_framework.title: UN Convention on Recovery Abroad of Maintenance 1956
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Convention on the Recovery Abroad of Maintenance adopted 20 June 1956 + effective 25 May 1957 — predecessor framework to Hague Convention 2007.

statutory_framework.title: Hague Convention 1996
statutory_framework.relevance: Hague Convention on the Protection of Children 1996 — concurrent applicability framework with Hague Convention 2007 via formal cooperation framework.

apex_courts.name: Permanent Bureau of the Hague Conference on Private International Law (HCCH)
apex_courts.role: Apex monitoring framework body for Hague Convention 2007 + Hague Protocol 2007.

apex_courts.name: National-level Central Authorities
apex_courts.role: National-level Central Authorities — formal Central-Authority-cooperation framework operational bodies.

professional_regulators.name: HCCH Secretariat
professional_regulators.role: Hague Convention 2007 + Hague Protocol 2007 implementation framework.

anonymisation_convention: Hague Convention 2007 + Hague Protocol 2007-related individual communications are anonymised per national-level court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: UN Convention on the Recovery Abroad of Maintenance
key_developments.description: UN Convention on the Recovery Abroad of Maintenance adopted 20 June 1956 — predecessor framework to Hague Convention 2007.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 2007 + Hague Protocol 2007 adopted
key_developments.description: Hague Convention on the International Recovery of Child Support and Other Forms of Family Maintenance + Hague Protocol on the Law Applicable to Maintenance Obligations adopted 23 November 2007 in The Hague — formal global binding-treaty framework.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 2007 enters force
key_developments.description: Hague Convention on the International Recovery of Child Support and Other Forms of Family Maintenance entered force 1 January 2013 — formal global binding-treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: Hague Protocol 2007 enters force
key_developments.description: Hague Protocol on the Law Applicable to Maintenance Obligations entered force 1 August 2013 — formal HCCH Protocol framework operational.

structural_findings: Hague Convention 2007 operates a supranational global binding-treaty framework — places Hague Convention 2007 in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern global binding-treaty framework operating specifically on cross-border-child-maintenance framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal-cross-border-child-maintenance-specific framework.
Central jurisdiction of formal HCCH post-Hague-1996 child-maintenance elaboration framework is structurally distinctive globally — Hague Convention 2007 framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Hague Protocol 2007 on Maintenance applicable-law framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global binding-treaty framework operating on formal-applicable-law-for-maintenance-obligations-specific framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Central Authority cooperation + Form Database + International Iberian Network IberRed framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global binding-treaty framework operating formal Form Database + International Iberian Network framework.
49 state party + 33 state party ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — substantial state party + non-state-party framework reflecting growing but limited adoption framework.
1956 UN Convention + 2007 Hague Convention + 2007 Hague Protocol + 2013 entry into force cross-border-child-maintenance-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.
Concurrent applicability framework with Hague Convention 1996 is structurally distinctive globally.

sources.title: Hague Conference on Private International Law (HCCH)

editorial_notes: Hague Convention 2007 + Hague Protocol 2007 jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global cross-border-child-maintenance + applicable-law-for-maintenance-obligations binding treaty meta-framework (Hague Convention 2007/2013 + Hague Protocol 2007/2013 + UN Convention on Recovery Abroad of Maintenance 1956 + Hague Convention 1996 concurrent applicability framework). Only modern global binding-treaty framework operating specifically on cross-border-child-maintenance framework globally + central jurisdiction of formal HCCH post-Hague-1996 child-maintenance elaboration framework + central jurisdiction of formal Hague Protocol 2007 on Maintenance applicable-law framework + central jurisdiction of formal Central Authority cooperation + Form Database + International Iberian Network IberRed framework + 49 state party + 33 state party ratification framework + 1956 UN Convention + 2007 Hague Convention + 2007 Hague Protocol + 2013 entry into force cross-border-child-maintenance-framework-development sequence + Concurrent applicability framework with Hague Convention 1996. Hague Conference children's-frameworks quartet complete (1980 + 1993 + 1996 + 2007).
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins global + supranational + global-cross-border-child-maintenance-binding-treaty cluster + Hague-Convention-2007-only-modern-global-binding-treaty-framework-operating-specifically-on-cross-border-child-maintenance-globally-distinctive + Hague-Protocol-2007-Maintenance-applicable-law + Form-Database + International-Iberian-Network-IberRed + Article-4-5-9-Central-Authority-cooperation + 49-state-party-33-state-party-ratification + UN-Convention-on-Recovery-Abroad-of-Maintenance-1956-predecessor + concurrent-applicability-with-Hague-Convention-1996 clusters within the corpus.
